name: TAT2 enhanced antiviral function in human CD8+ T cells ex vivo
summary: Human-cell data support mechanistic plausibility for telomerase activators in immune aging contexts.
statement: In cultured CD8+ T lymphocytes from HIV-infected donors, TAT2 modestly slowed telomere shortening, increased proliferative potential, and enhanced antiviral activity through a telomerase-sensitive mechanism.
population_or_model: Human CD8+ T cells from HIV-infected donors cultured ex vivo
quantitative_note: The abstract reports that antiviral enhancement was abrogated by a telomerase inhibitor.
caveats: This is ex vivo human-cell evidence, not in vivo clinical benefit.
The donor disease context may not generalize to healthy aging.
The intervention is not the same as currently marketed TA-65 products or AAV-hTERT gene therapy.
